blocks.content: A Connection (pushtopics__Connection__c) defines the authentication configuration used to connect to a Salesforce org. A Connection can act as:

children.content: A Source for retrieving data
A Target for performing actions

blocks.content: The diagram below illustrates how DSP manages authentication. Refer to the sections that follow for detailed information about each supported connection configuration.

children.content: The "Is Current Org?" checkbox is a straightforward field on the Connection object. Once checked, DSP operates without requiring credentials.

It conducts queries and DML operations based on the currently logged-in user's context, indicating that the connection pertains to the current org where Data Sync Pro is installed. All security settings applicable to that user are enforced during executions.
For a more detailed walkthrough, refer to the video below:
DSP supports authentication using Username and Password (with Security Token) via Salesforce Named Credentials.
The steps below outline the setup process.
Establish a Named Credential:
URL: Use `https://test.salesforce.com` for sandbox or `https://login.salesforce.com` for production.
Identity Type: Select `Named Principal`.
Authentication Protocol: Choose `Password Authentication`.
Username: Enter the main username of the connected organization.
Password: Input the user's password combined with the security token. If not applicable, omit it.
Create a DSP Connection Record: Name it after the Named Credential you've just set up.
Test Connection: Verify that the setup works as expected.
DSP supports OAuth 2.0 authentication using Salesforce security features, including:
Connected App
Auth. Provider
Named Credential
The steps below outline the setup process.
Create a Connected App in the target org

Navigate to the App Manager within Org B to create a new Connected App. Ensure you activate the OAuth Flow. Initially, use a temporary URL for the Callback URL. In the "scope" section, select "refresh_token" and include any additional scopes required for the integration user's data access.
Create an Auth. Provider in the org where DSP is installed
Copy and paste the Consumer Key and Consumer Secret from Org B's Connected App to the Auth. Provider.
Authorize Endpoint URL: Use `https://test.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize` for sandbox or `https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize` for production.
Token Endpoint URL: Use `https://test.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token` for sandbox or `https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token` for production.
Default Scopes: Set `refresh_token` and any additional scopes. Use space to separate.
Update the Callback URL in the target org
Upon save of the Auth. Provider, a Callback URL will be generated. Update the Connected App's Callback URL to match the value.
Create a Named Credential in the org where DSP is installed
URL: the domain URL of Org B. This can be found in "My Domain Settings".
Identity Type: Select `Named Principal`.
Authentication Protocol: Choose `OAuth 2.0`.
Auth. Provider: Pick the one you just set up.
Scope: Use `refresh_token full` or any scopes you need.
Enable "Generate Authorization Header", "Allow Merge Fields in HTTP Header", and "Allow Merge Fields in HTTP Body".
Test the connection
